perturbation.original_text: "LMG Tools" means the LMG-developed computer programs identified in Exhibit A, including, where applicable object code (including microcode) and/or source code, that are provided or to be provided by LMG pursuant to this Agreement. The definition of LMG Tools also includes any enhancements, translations, modifications, updates, releases, or other changes to LMG Tools.
perturbation.changed_text: "LMG Tools" means the LMG-developed computer programs identified in Exhibit A, including, where applicable object code (including microcode) and/or source code, that are provided or to be provided by LMG pursuant to this Agreement. The definition of LMG Tools **specifically excludes** any enhancements, translations, modifications, updates, releases, or other changes to LMG Tools.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction in the definition of 'LMG Tools'. Initially, the definition includes any modifications, updates, or enhancements. With this perturbation, it specifically excludes them, creating ambiguity about what constitutes 'LMG Tools' under the agreement. This could create internal contradiction in Article III where the LMG is required to provide to TAG all upgrades, modifications, improvements, enhancements, extensions, and other changes to LMG Tools developed by LMG.
perturbation.contradicted_text: LMG shall provide the following with respect to the LMG Tools, CSC Software, Other Third Party Software and Documentation for the period specified in Exhibit A: LMG shall provide (or will cause the third party vendor to provide) to TAG all error corrections and all operational and support assistance necessary to cause the LMG Tools, CSC Software and Other Third Party Software to perform in accordance with their Applicable Specifications. LMG shall also provide remedial support designed to provide a by-pass or temporary fix to a defect until the defect can be permanently corrected. LMG shall provide (or will cause the third party vendor to provide) to TAG all upgrades, modifications, improvements, enhancements, extensions, and other changes to LMG Tools developed by LMG. LMG shall provide (or will cause the third party vendor to provide) to TAG any revisions to the existing Documentation developed for the LMG Tools, CSC Software and Other Third Party Software or necessary to reflect all corrections, updates, upgrades, modifications, improvements, enhancements, extensions or other changes thereto. LMG shall provide training to TAG with respect to the use of the LMG Tools, Other Third Party Software and Documentation as reasonably requested by TAG.
perturbation.justification: The changed text creates a strong in-text contradiction. The original definition of 'LMG Tools' includes enhancements, modifications, updates, etc. The changed text explicitly excludes these items. Article III, the 'Contradicted Text', states that LMG shall provide TAG all upgrades, modifications, improvements, enhancements, extensions, and other changes to LMG Tools developed by LMG. This creates a contradiction because Article III requires LMG to provide items that, according to the definition in the changed text, are not part of the 'LMG Tools'. This contradicts the terms set out in the contract, making enforcement unclear and triggering condition 1, Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: LMG shall exercise commercially reasonable efforts to maintain the employment of the LMG employees identified in Exhibit B ("Retained Employees") for the estimated retention period specified in Exhibit B or such other period as TAG may reasonably request ("Retention Period").
perturbation.changed_text: LMG shall exercise commercially reasonable efforts to maintain the employment of the LMG employees identified in Exhibit B ("Retained Employees") for the estimated retention period specified in Exhibit B or such other period as TAG may reasonably request ("Retention Period"). **However, LMG is not obligated to maintain the employment of any Retained Employee beyond their initially agreed-upon retention period as listed in Exhibit B.**
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation introduces a contradiction regarding the 'Retention Period' of 'Retained Employees'. The original text states that the period can be extended by TAG's request. The added clause limits this possibility, stating that there is no obligation to retain employees beyond the period in Exhibit B. This directly contradicts the initial allowance for TAG to extend the Retention Period and create internal contradiction if TAG requests for extension of the Retention Period, LMG may refuse to do it.
perturbation.contradicted_text: LMG shall exercise commercially reasonable efforts to maintain the employment of the LMG employees identified in Exhibit B ("Retained Employees") for the estimated retention period specified in Exhibit B or such other period as TAG may reasonably request ("Retention Period"). LMG shall cause them to devote the same amount of time and attention to the performance of the services under this Agreement as they presently devote to such services. In all events, LMG shall retain a sufficient number of qualified personnel to perform the LMG Services. The Retained Employees shall be employees of LMG for all purposes. LMG shall be solely responsible for funding and distributing benefits under the benefit plans in which the Retained Employees participate and for paying any compensation and remitting any income, disability, withholding and other employment taxes for such Retained Employees. For clarity, the foregoing shall not affect TAG's obligation to reimburse LMG for the costs specifically identified as reimbursable by TAG during the period, and under the terms and conditions, set forth in Sections 4.1 and 4.2 of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text allows for TAG to request an extension to the retention period of LMG employees, while the changed text explicitly limits this possibility by stating that LMG is not obligated to maintain employment beyond the initially agreed-upon period in Exhibit B. This creates a direct conflict, as TAG's ability to request an extension is negated by LMG's lack of obligation to grant it, thus leading to an in-text contradiction with the contradicted text, which assumes TAG can reasonably request for an extension.

perturbation.original_text: Termination for Cause. In the event that either party materially defaults in the performance of its duties or obligations set forth in this Agreement, and such default is not cured within thirty (30) days after written notice is given to the defaulting party specifying the default, then the party not in default may, by giving written notice thereof to the defaulting party, terminate the Agreement as of a date specified in such notice of termination.
perturbation.changed_text: Termination for Cause. In the event that either party materially defaults in the performance of its duties or obligations set forth in this Agreement, and such default is not cured within thirty (30) days after written notice is given to the defaulting party specifying the default, then the party not in default may, by giving written notice thereof to the defaulting party, terminate the Agreement as of a date specified in such notice of termination. **However, termination for cause is only permitted if the material default results in direct financial loss to the non-defaulting party greater than $1,000,000.**
perturbation.explanation: This modification adds a financial threshold for 'Termination for Cause'. While the original clause allows termination for any material default, the added stipulation restricts this right to cases where the financial loss exceeds $1,000,000. This creates a contradiction: some material defaults (that do not meet the financial loss threshold) cannot be used as a reason for the Termination of the contract, even though they are considered a material default. This contradiction leads to an in-text disagreement with Article V where LMG warranties that each item of LMG Tools and, to LMG's knowledge, the CSC Software and Other Third Party Software (i) shall be free from defects and (ii) shall function properly under ordinary Use and operate in conformance with its Applicable Specifications and Documentation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: LMG represents and warrants that: The LMG Tools, CSC Software, Other Third Party Software and Documentation are and shall be free and clear of all liens and encumbrances, and TAG shall be entitled to Use them without disturbance; No portion of the LMG Tools, CSC Software and Other Third Party Software contains, at the time of delivery, any "back door," "time bomb," "Trojan horse," "worm," "drop dead device," "virus," or other computer software routines or hardware components designed to (i) permit access or Use of such software or TAG's computer systems by LMG or a third party not authorized by this Agreement, or (ii) disable, damage or erase the software or data; The LMG Tools, CSC Software and Other Third Party Software and the design thereof shall not contain preprogrammed preventative routines or similar devices which prevent TAG from exercising the rights granted to TAG under this Agreement or from utilizing the software for the purpose for which they were designed; and Each item of LMG Tools and, to LMG's knowledge, the CSC Software and Other Third Party Software (i) shall be free from defects and (ii) shall function properly under ordinary Use and operate in conformance with its Applicable Specifications and Documentation. During the period specified in Exhibit A, LMG will provide warranty service to TAG at no additional charge and will include all LMG Services or replacement software necessary to enable LMG to comply with the warranties set forth in this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original 'Termination for Cause' clause allows termination for any material default, whereas the changed text introduces a financial threshold of $1,000,000. This directly contradicts the warranty provided by LMG regarding the functionality and defect-free nature of the LMG Tools, CSC Software, and Other Third Party Software. If a defect occurs that constitutes a material default but does not result in financial loss exceeding $1,000,000, TAG cannot terminate the agreement for cause under the revised clause, even though the warranty is breached. This creates a direct conflict and uncertainty, as TAG's rights under the warranty are effectively limited by the new financial threshold for termination.
